_note: The owner's running ledger of trading theses, captured verbatim-in-spirit as he dumps them. Each is a HYPOTHESIS, not a rule: the Phase-4 proving harness (src/validation/registry.py) registers it, the discovery layer builds the signal that tests it, and it earns its way onto proposal cards only after surviving out-of-sample (decision #50/#63). `testable_signal` sketches what the engine must compute to score it; `builds` names the layer that would carry it; `status` is idea|scoping|building|registered|validated|killed. This file is the human capture surface — edit freely; nothing here trades until validated.

hypotheses.statement: In a flat market, capital doesn't exit — it rotates by exhaustion. When a leading sector/instrument is extended AND its volatility rises (choppy top), money flees it and hunts whatever has been stagnant/basing; that laggard then breaks out. Buy the BASING laggard WHEN the former leader is extended + going high-vol, not just because the laggard is cheap.
hypotheses.mechanism: leader extension + rising leader-volatility -> capital flight -> inflow to a low-vol basing laggard -> laggard breakout (volume/delivery confirming).
examples: bonds + financial services becoming the destination as the broad index has gone ~2 years without real upside
hypotheses.testable_signal: A rotation radar: rank sectors/instruments by trailing return (multi-horizon) AND realized volatility. Flag LEADERS = high trailing return + rising vol (extension). Flag LAGGARDS = flat trailing return + low/compressing vol (basing). Fire when a flagged laggard breaks its base range with volume/delivery-% confirmation while >=1 leader is simultaneously extended-high-vol. Score: does the laggard breakout outperform over the next N weeks?
confirmation_layers: FII/DII flows rotating out of the leader group into the laggard group (flows_tracker)
delivery % spike on the laggard breakout day
hypotheses.builds: a new sector/instrument relative-strength + volatility-regime tracker (Phase-5 rotation radar) feeding daily_context; registered as a candidate_pattern once the radar emits it
caveats: needs a sector->constituents map (partly in config/entity_groups.json + config/sector_links.json; needs a broader sector index set)
'basing then breakout' must be defined precisely (range compression + a real range-break, not noise) or it over-fires
survivorship: only patterns that survive the walk-forward trial reach a card
